seo.title: Building Content Generation with BeeAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build content generation with BeeAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: BeeAI, IBM's open-source framework, enables robust AI agents for content generation with built-in memory, tools, and observability. For content creation use cases, it powers LLM-driven workflows enforcing brand voice, fact-checking, and editorial guidelines. Vex guardrails are essential to mitigate risks like unbounded memory growth, tool injection, and model output inconsistencies that threaten production reliability.

architectureOverview.heading: Content Generation Architecture with BeeAI
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture centers on a BeeAI agent orchestrating content creation through a chain of LLM calls. User prompts trigger the agent, which leverages memory for conversation history and brand guidelines. Tools handle fact-checking via web search APIs and enforce editorial rules through structured validation.

Key components include: persistent agent memory storing brand voice profiles and past outputs, tool integrations for SEO analysis and plagiarism checks, and an event bus for observability. The agent serializes state between sessions to maintain context across long-form content generation.

Output flows through verification layers ensuring brand consistency, factual accuracy, and SEO compliance before final delivery.

risks.name: Unbounded Agent Memory Growth
risks.description: Long content generation sessions accumulate conversation history without truncation, causing OOM errors and slow inference.
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors memory token usage and enforces summarization checkpoints

risks.name: Tool Injection via Malicious Prompts
risks.description: User prompts exploit tool validation gaps to execute unauthorized API calls or data exfiltration.
risks.mitigation: Vex input sanitization and tool call allowlisting prevents injection attacks

risks.name: Brand Voice Drift
risks.description: LLM outputs gradually deviate from stored brand guidelines during iterative refinement loops.
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic similarity checks against brand voice embeddings

risks.name: Fact-Check Tool Failures
risks.description: Web search tools return stale data or fail silently, propagating factual errors to final content.
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies tool responses against multiple sources and flags inconsistencies

implementation.title: Basic BeeAI Content Agent Setup
implementation.description: Initialize BeeAI agent with memory and content generation tools for brand-aligned writing.
implementation.code:
from beeai import Agent, Memory
from beeai.tools import Tool

# Brand voice memory
memory = Memory(persist=True)
memory.add('brand_guidelines', {
    'tone': 'professional',
    'voice': 'authoritative yet approachable',
    'length': '800-1200 words'
})

# Content generation agent
content_agent = Agent(
    name="ContentCreator",
    model="granite-3b",
    memory=memory,
    tools=[
        Tool(name="fact_check", func=web_search),
        Tool(name="seo_analyze", func=seo_checker)
    ]
)

result = content_agent.run("Write blog post about AI ethics for enterprise leaders")

implementation.title: Brand Voice Enforcement Tool
implementation.description: Custom tool validates generated content against stored brand guidelines using semantic similarity.
implementation.code:
from beeai.tools import Tool
import numpy as np
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer

model = SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2')
brand_embeddings = memory.get('brand_embeddings')

def validate_brand_voice(content: str) -> dict:
    content_emb = model.encode(content)
    similarities = np.dot(brand_embeddings, content_emb) / (
        np.linalg.norm(brand_embeddings) * np.linalg.norm(content_emb)
    )
    return {
        'consistent': np.mean(similarities) > 0.85,
        'similarity_score': float(np.mean(similarities))
    }

brand_tool = Tool(name="brand_check", func=validate_brand_voice)

implementation.title: Fact-Checking Integration
implementation.description: Integrate real-time web search for factual verification during content generation.

implementation.title: SEO Compliance Checker
implementation.description: Tool ensures generated content meets SEO best practices and keyword targets.

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex Event Listener
vexIntegration.description: Hook Vex into BeeAI's event system to monitor all agent interactions and enforce guardrails.
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Vex
from beeai import Agent
from vex.guardrails import MemoryGuardrail, ToolGuardrail

# Initialize Vex client
vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-key")

# Create guardrails for content generation
memory_guard = MemoryGuardrail(
    max_tokens=50000,
    summarization_trigger=40000
)
tool_guard = ToolGuardrail(
    allowed_tools=["fact_check", "seo_analyze", "brand_check"],
    input_sanitization=True
)

# Register Vex as BeeAI event listener
content_agent.vex_client = vex
content_agent.add_event_listener(
    "*",
    lambda event: vex.record(
        session_id=content_agent.session_id,
        event_type=event.type,
        payload=event.payload,
        guardrails=[memory_guard, tool_guard]
    )
)

vexIntegration.title: Brand Consistency Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Real-time semantic verification ensures content adheres to brand voice guidelines.
vexIntegration.code:
from vex.guardrails import SemanticGuardrail

brand_guardrail = SemanticGuardrail(
    name="brand_consistency",
    reference_texts=[
        memory.get('brand_guidelines')['examples'],
        "Our voice is authoritative yet approachable"
    ],
    min_similarity=0.85,
    action_on_fail="revise",
    revision_prompt="Rewrite maintaining our brand voice: {{reference_texts}}"
)

# Attach to agent
content_agent.guardrails.append(brand_guardrail)

# Vex will automatically:
# 1. Compare outputs against brand embeddings
# 2. Block inconsistent content
# 3. Auto-revise with brand-aligned prompts

vexIntegration.title: Factual Accuracy & Memory Management
vexIntegration.description: Combined guardrail prevents hallucination and unbounded memory growth in long sessions.
vexIntegration.code:
from vex.guardrails import FactCheckGuardrail, MemoryGuardrail

# Fact-checking with source verification
fact_guard = FactCheckGuardrail(
    sources=["google", "wikipedia", "enterprise_db"],
    min_source_agreement=0.7,
    block_threshold=0.3
)

# Memory management for long content generation
memory_guard = MemoryGuardrail(
    max_context_tokens=45000,
    summarization_model="granite-3b",
    keep_recent_turns=5
)

# Bundle guardrails
content_suite = vex.create_guardrail_suite([
    fact_guard,
    memory_guard,
    SemanticGuardrail(name="tone_adherence", min_similarity=0.8)
])

# Apply to agent
content_agent.vex_suite = content_suite

# Now agent automatically:
# - Verifies facts against 3+ sources
# - Summarizes memory at 45k tokens
# - Enforces tone consistency

cta.heading: Secure Your BeeAI Content Generation
cta.description: Deploy Vex guardrails today to ensure brand-consistent, factually accurate content at production scale.
